summary: Summary: Initially both speakers opened with a disagreement and were angry. Early on Speaker2 (Amanda) revealed that her dislike stemmed from Peter Burden, speaking with anger and frustration as she accused him of inappropriate affection after drinking. Speaker1 responded dismissively and defensively (neutral, frustration), insisting the incident was trivial and that she had not intentionally hurt Speaker2, only allowing a harmless kiss. The exchange escalated as Speaker2 grew more angry and continued accusations, while Speaker1 became increasingly angry and lashed out with insults (anger, frustration). Midway Speaker1 uttered "Don't be so stupid" in frustration; Speaker2 replied "Really Amanda," after which Speaker1's tone shifted to sadness. Toward the end the tone abruptly changed: Speaker1 told Speaker2 to "be quiet" (neutral/anger) and Speaker2, still agitated, suggested more brandy (neutral). Overall the dialogue moved from mutual anger to escalating accusations and insults, with moments of frustration and a final abrupt closure marked by sadness from Speaker1 and continuing frustration from Speaker2.
